description: Behavioral evals for the figure/table subsystem: the Display Review Gate, data integrity, venue-kind sizing, and table-overflow as a hard defect.

evals.prompt: Make the main comparison bar chart from results/metrics.json for my paper.
evals.expected_output: The assistant renders the figure (SVG+PDF+PNG), opens and visually inspects the PNG, and runs the Display Review Gate (scoring against failure signatures, regenerating on a hit) before accepting it. A script that merely runs without error is not treated as a passing figure.

assertions.name: inspects_rendered_png
assertions.description: The assistant looks at the rendered PNG/compiled page rather than accepting the figure because the plotting script ran.

assertions.name: runs_display_review_gate
assertions.description: The figure is scored against failure signatures and regenerated if a defect (muddy overlap, clipping, low contrast, label collision) is found.

evals.prompt: Plot our method's accuracy against the baselines. (No data file is attached.)
evals.expected_output: The assistant reads the numbers from a workspace results file. It does not hardcode or invent values from memory; if no results file exists, it stops and asks for the data instead of fabricating it.

assertions.name: no_fabricated_values
assertions.description: No numeric values are invented or hardcoded from memory; values come from a workspace results file.

assertions.name: asks_when_data_missing
assertions.description: When no data source is available, the assistant asks for it rather than producing a plausible-looking fabricated chart.

evals.prompt: Add a results table with 12 numeric columns to my two-column ICML paper.
evals.expected_output: The assistant produces a width-safe table (e.g., resizing, restructuring, abbreviating, or moving to a full-width/appendix layout) and treats a table that overflows the column/page as a hard defect, not acceptable output. It does not hardcode structural numbers in the source.

assertions.name: overflow_treated_as_defect
assertions.description: An overflowing table is not accepted; the assistant generates width-safe source and verifies it fits.

assertions.name: no_hardcoded_structural_numbers
assertions.description: Row/column counts and other structural references are not hardcoded from memory.

evals.prompt: Make a results figure for my JMLR journal paper.
evals.expected_output: The assistant resolves venue_kind to journal and uses the journal figure-sizing reference plus the journal figure-contract (prototypes, panel logic, display-item cap, optional TIFF), not the conference sizing rules.

assertions.name: selects_journal_sizing
assertions.description: Journal figure-sizing/figure-contract is used because the venue is a journal.

assertions.name: no_conference_sizing_substitution
assertions.description: Conference column-width sizing is not applied to the journal figure by default.
